column2:
polusi / pencemaran
The presence of harmful substances in the environment.
Related: pollute, pollutant, air pollution, water pollution.
Air pollution in big cities causes serious health problems.

penggundulan hutan
The action of clearing a wide area of trees.
Related: deforest, forest loss, logging.
Deforestation in the Amazon threatens countless species.

terbarukan
Able to be replaced naturally; not running out.
Related: renewable energy, renewable resources, non-renewable.
Wind and solar are renewable sources of energy.

ekosistem
A community of living things interacting with their environment.
Related: ecology, ecological balance, marine ecosystem.
Coral reefs are among the most diverse ecosystems on Earth.

keanekaragaman hayati
The variety of plant and animal life in an area.
Related: biological diversity, species, conservation.
Tropical rainforests are rich in biodiversity.

emisi / gas buangan
Gases released into the atmosphere, often from burning fuel.
Related: emit, carbon emissions, greenhouse emissions.
Governments are working to reduce carbon emissions globally.

konservasi / pelestarian
Protection of plants, animals, and natural areas.
Related: conserve, conservationist, wildlife conservation.
Conservation efforts have saved many species from extinction.

perubahan iklim
Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns.
Related: global warming, climate crisis, climate action.
Climate change is causing sea levels to rise rapidly.

mendaur ulang
To convert waste into reusable material.
Related: recycling, recyclable, reuse, reduce.
We should recycle paper, plastic, and glass at home.

bahan bakar fosil
Natural fuels like coal, oil, and gas formed from ancient organisms.
Related: coal, petroleum, non-renewable energy.
Burning fossil fuels is the main cause of global warming.

lapisan ozon
A layer in the atmosphere that protects Earth from UV rays.
Related: ozone depletion, stratosphere, CFCs.
The ozone layer absorbs most of the sun's harmful radiation.

gas rumah kaca
A gas that traps heat in the atmosphere.
Related: carbon dioxide, methane, greenhouse effect.
Carbon dioxide is the most common greenhouse gas.

berkelanjutan
Able to continue over time without harming the environment.
Related: sustainability, sustainable development.
Solar power is a sustainable source of energy.

terancam punah
At risk of becoming extinct.
Related: endangered species, extinct, threatened.
The Sumatran tiger is critically endangered.

habitat / tempat tinggal alami
The natural home of an animal or plant.
Related: natural habitat, habitat loss, ecosystem.
Pandas live in bamboo forests, their natural habitat.

kurikulum
The subjects taught in a school or course.
Related: syllabus, curricula, national curriculum.
The school updated its curriculum to include coding.

beasiswa
Financial aid awarded to students for academic merit.
Related: scholar, grant, bursary, full scholarship.
She won a scholarship to study at Oxford University.

biaya kuliah / les
Money paid for instruction, especially at university.
Related: tuition fees, private tuition, tutor.
University tuition fees have risen sharply in recent years.

kejuruan
Related to skills training for a specific job.
Related: vocational training, vocation, vocational school.
Vocational courses prepare students for practical careers.

mahasiswa S1
A university student studying for a first degree.
Related: undergraduate degree, bachelor's, freshman.
As an undergraduate, he studied economics for four years.

mahasiswa pascasarjana
A student studying for an advanced degree after a bachelor's.
Related: master's, PhD, postgraduate studies.
She is a postgraduate student researching renewable energy.

kemampuan baca tulis
The ability to read and write.
Related: literate, illiteracy, digital literacy.
Literacy rates have improved across developing countries.

pembelajaran jarak jauh
Education delivered remotely, often online.
Related: online learning, e-learning, remote study.
Distance learning became popular during the pandemic.

tekanan dari teman sebaya
Influence from people of the same age group.
Related: peers, social pressure, conformity.
Teenagers often face peer pressure to fit in at school.

akademis
Related to education, especially at universities.
Related: academic year, academia, academic performance.
His academic achievements earned him a place at Cambridge.

kuliah / ceramah
A formal talk given to students.
Related: lecturer, lecture hall, attend a lecture.
Students take notes during the professor's lecture.

silabus
An outline of topics covered in a course.
Related: curriculum, course outline, syllabi.
The syllabus lists all the topics for this semester.

kualifikasi / ijazah
A certificate or skill showing ability or knowledge.
Related: qualify, qualified, professional qualification.
He has the qualifications needed for the teaching job.

plagiarisme / penjiplakan
Copying someone else's work and claiming it as your own.
Related: plagiarize, plagiarist, academic misconduct.
Plagiarism is a serious offense at every university.

putus sekolah / berhenti kuliah
To leave school or college before finishing.
Related: dropout, quit, withdraw.
Many students drop out of university due to financial problems.

obesitas / kegemukan
The condition of being seriously overweight.
Related: obese, overweight, body mass index.
Childhood obesity has become a major public health issue.

kurang gerak / banyak duduk
Involving little physical activity.
Related: sedentary lifestyle, inactive, desk job.
A sedentary lifestyle increases the risk of heart disease.

nutrisi / gizi
The process of getting food needed for health and growth.
Related: nutritious, nutrient, balanced diet.
Good nutrition is essential for children's development.

sistem kekebalan tubuh
The body's defense against infections and disease.
Related: immunity, immune response, immunize.
Vitamin C helps strengthen your immune system.

kesehatan mental
A person's emotional and psychological well-being.
Related: mental illness, anxiety, depression, well-being.
More schools are addressing mental health among students.

kesejahteraan
The state of being healthy, comfortable, and happy.
Related: wellness, welfare, physical well-being.
Exercise improves both physical and mental well-being.

mendiagnosis
To identify an illness or problem.
Related: diagnosis, diagnostic, undiagnosed.
Doctors diagnosed her with diabetes last year.

menular
Spread from one person to another by contact.
Related: infectious, contagion, communicable disease.
The flu is highly contagious during winter months.

kardiovaskular / terkait jantung dan pembuluh darah
Related to the heart and blood vessels.
Related: heart disease, cardiovascular system, circulation.
Regular exercise lowers the risk of cardiovascular disease.

kronis / menahun
Lasting a long time; persistent.
Related: chronic illness, chronic pain, long-term.
She suffers from chronic back pain after the accident.

vaksin
A substance that protects against disease.
Related: vaccination, vaccinate, immunization.
The COVID-19 vaccine saved millions of lives worldwide.

masa hidup / rentang umur
The length of time a person or animal lives.
Related: longevity, life cycle, average lifespan.
Modern medicine has extended the human lifespan.

sanitasi
Conditions related to clean water and waste disposal.
Related: sanitary, hygiene, public sanitation.
Poor sanitation leads to the spread of disease.

kebersihan / higiene
Practices that maintain health and prevent disease.
Related: hygienic, personal hygiene, oral hygiene.
Washing hands is a basic rule of good hygiene.

angka harapan hidup
The average period a person is expected to live.
Related: longevity, mortality, lifespan.
Life expectancy in Japan is among the highest in the world.

kecerdasan buatan
Computer systems that can perform human-like tasks.
Related: AI, machine learning, automation.
Artificial intelligence is transforming many industries today.

otomatisasi
Use of machines to do tasks once done by humans.
Related: automate, automated, robotics.
Automation has replaced many factory jobs.

kesenjangan digital
The gap between those who have technology access and those who don't.
Related: digital gap, internet access, inequality.
The digital divide leaves rural students at a disadvantage.

keamanan siber
Protection of computer systems from digital attacks.
Related: hacking, data breach, online safety.
Companies invest heavily in cybersecurity to protect data.

media sosial
Online platforms for sharing content and connecting with people.
Related: Facebook, Instagram, social network.
Many people spend hours on social media every day.

perangkat / gawai
A small electronic device with a useful function.
Related: device, tool, electronics.
Teenagers love buying the latest gadgets.

inovasi
A new method, idea, or product.
Related: innovate, innovative, breakthrough.
Technological innovation drives economic growth.

realitas virtual
A computer-generated simulated environment.
Related: VR, augmented reality, immersive.
Virtual reality is now used in education and training.

bioteknologi
Technology based on biology, often for medicine or agriculture.
Related: biotechnology, genetic engineering, bioscience.
Biotech companies are developing new cancer treatments.

perdagangan elektronik
Buying and selling goods online.
Related: online shopping, digital marketplace, e-business.
E-commerce has changed how we shop forever.

algoritma
A set of rules followed by a computer to solve a problem.
Related: code, programming, computation.
Search engines use algorithms to rank web pages.

ponsel pintar
A mobile phone with advanced computing features.
Related: mobile, app, touchscreen.
Smartphones have replaced cameras for most people.

komputasi awan
Storing and accessing data over the internet.
Related: cloud storage, server, online services.
Cloud computing allows employees to work from anywhere.

privasi daring
Protection of personal information on the internet.
Related: data protection, cybersecurity, privacy settings.
Many users are worried about their online privacy.

waktu menatap layar
The amount of time spent using a screen device.
Related: device usage, digital health, eye strain.
Excessive screen time can affect children's sleep.

pekerjaan / profesi
A person's job or profession.
Related: profession, employment, career.
Please state your occupation on the application form.

tenaga kerja
All the people working in a country or industry.
Related: labor force, employees, staff.
Women now make up half of the workforce.

pengangguran
The state of being without a paid job.
Related: unemployed, jobless, unemployment rate.
Unemployment rates rose sharply during the recession.

gaji
Fixed regular payment for work, usually monthly.
Related: wage, income, paycheck.
She earns a competitive salary as a software engineer.

promosi / kenaikan jabatan
A move to a higher position at work.
Related: promote, promoted, career advancement.
He received a promotion after five years at the company.

beban kerja
The amount of work to be done.
Related: heavy workload, work pressure, tasks.
The new project has increased my workload significantly.

keseimbangan kerja dan kehidupan
Balance between work and personal life.
Related: lifestyle, leisure, time management.
Many young workers value work-life balance over salary.

masa pensiun
The period when one stops working due to age.
Related: retire, retired, pension.
He plans to travel the world after retirement.

lepas / pekerja mandiri
Working for different clients without a permanent contract.
Related: freelancer, self-employed, contractor.
Many designers prefer freelance work for its flexibility.

pemberi kerja / atasan
A person or company that hires workers.
Related: employee, employment, hire.
Good employers invest in training their staff.

wirausahawan / pengusaha
Someone who starts and runs their own business.
Related: entrepreneurship, startup, business owner.
Successful entrepreneurs take calculated risks.

magang
A short period of work experience offered by employers.
Related: intern, work experience, trainee.
She did an internship at a marketing firm last summer.

pemutusan hubungan kerja / PHK
Loss of a job because it is no longer needed.
Related: redundant, lay off, dismissal.
Hundreds of staff faced redundancy when the factory closed.

jalur karier
The progression of jobs in a person's working life.
Related: career, professional development, job ladder.
Choosing the right career path can be difficult.

jam kerja
The hours during which one works.
Related: work schedule, overtime, shift.
Long working hours can damage your health.

tradisi
A custom or belief passed down through generations.
Related: traditional, custom, heritage.
Eating ketupat is a tradition during Eid in Indonesia.

kesenjangan generasi
Differences in values between younger and older people.
Related: generations, age gap, misunderstanding.
Technology has widened the generation gap in many families.

urbanisasi
The growth of cities as people move from the countryside.
Related: urban, urbanize, city growth.
Rapid urbanization has caused housing shortages.

imigrasi
The act of coming to live permanently in another country.
Related: immigrant, migrate, immigration policy.
Immigration brings cultural diversity to many nations.

identitas budaya
The sense of belonging to a particular culture.
Related: heritage, ethnicity, cultural background.
Language is an important part of cultural identity.

stereotip
A widely held but oversimplified idea about a group.
Related: stereotypical, prejudice, generalization.
We should challenge stereotypes about gender roles.

kesetaraan gender
Equal rights and opportunities for all genders.
Related: equality, women's rights, feminism.
Gender equality in the workplace remains a global goal.

demografi / kelompok populasi
A particular section of a population.
Related: demographics, population, age group.
The product is aimed at a younger demographic.

multikultural
Including people from many different cultures.
Related: diversity, multiculturalism, cultural mix.
London is a vibrant multicultural city.

kelas sosial
A group within society based on economic status.
Related: middle class, working class, social hierarchy.
Social class still affects access to good education.

populasi yang menua
A society where the proportion of elderly is increasing.
Related: elderly, demographic shift, retirement.
Japan is dealing with the challenges of an ageing population.

sensor / penyensoran
Control of what information can be shared publicly.
Related: censor, freedom of speech, restriction.
Censorship of the internet is common in some countries.

gaya hidup
The way a person or group lives.
Related: way of life, healthy lifestyle, modern lifestyle.
A healthy lifestyle includes exercise and good nutrition.

komunitas / masyarakat
A group of people living in the same area or sharing interests.
Related: communal, neighborhood, society.
The local community organized a clean-up event.

integrasi / pembauran
The process of combining people or groups into a society.
Related: integrate, social integration, inclusion.
Successful integration of immigrants benefits society.

tujuan / destinasi
The place to which someone is travelling.
Related: travel destination, tourist destination, arrival.
Bali is a popular destination for international tourists.

akomodasi / penginapan
A place to stay during travel.
Related: lodging, hotel, hostel, stay.
We booked our accommodation online before the trip.

pertukaran budaya
Sharing of customs and ideas between different cultures.
Related: exchange program, cross-cultural, diversity.
Travel offers wonderful opportunities for cultural exchange.

ekowisata
Tourism focused on natural environments and conservation.
Related: sustainable tourism, ecotourist, nature travel.
Eco-tourism helps protect endangered habitats.

traveling hemat dengan ransel
Travelling on a low budget while carrying a backpack.
Related: backpacker, budget travel, hostel.
He went backpacking across Southeast Asia for six months.

jadwal perjalanan / rencana perjalanan
A planned route or schedule for a trip.
Related: travel plan, schedule, route.
Our itinerary includes three days in Tokyo and two in Kyoto.

jalan-jalan melihat tempat wisata
Visiting interesting places as a tourist.
Related: tourist attraction, landmark, tour.
We spent the afternoon sightseeing in old Jakarta.

musim ramai / musim puncak
The busiest time of year for tourism.
Related: high season, off-season, tourist season.
Hotel prices double during the peak season.

visa
Official permission to enter a country.
Related: passport, immigration, tourist visa.
Indonesians need a visa to visit most European countries.

bea cukai
The official department that checks goods entering a country.
Related: customs officer, declare, duty-free.
You must declare expensive items at customs.

ekspatriat / orang asing yang tinggal di negara lain
A person living outside their native country.
Related: expat, immigrant, foreign worker.
Many expatriates work in the oil industry in the Middle East.

mata uang
The money used in a particular country.
Related: exchange rate, foreign currency, banknote.
The local currency in Indonesia is the rupiah.

kelelahan akibat perbedaan waktu
Tiredness after a long flight across time zones.
Related: time zone, long-haul flight, fatigue.
I had terrible jet lag after flying from London to Sydney.

cinderamata / oleh-oleh
An object kept as a reminder of a place visited.
Related: keepsake, memento, gift shop.
She bought a small souvenir from every city she visited.

masakan lokal
Traditional food of a particular region.
Related: traditional food, regional dish, gastronomy.
Trying local cuisine is one of the joys of travelling.

peraturan perundang-undangan
Laws made by a government.
Related: legislate, law, legal, bill.
New legislation will reduce plastic waste nationwide.

pemilihan umum / pemilu
The process of choosing leaders by voting.
Related: elect, voter, ballot, candidate.
The general election will be held next October.

demokrasi
A system where citizens vote to choose their government.
Related: democratic, democrat, free election.
Democracy depends on an informed and active citizenry.

korupsi
Dishonest behavior by those in power, often for personal gain.
Related: corrupt, bribery, anti-corruption.
Corruption among officials slows down economic development.

pencegah / penghalang
Something that discourages an action, especially crime.
Related: deter, prevent, discourage.
Heavy fines act as a deterrent to speeding drivers.

rehabilitasi
The process of helping someone return to a normal life.
Related: rehabilitate, recovery, reform.
Prisons should focus on rehabilitation, not just punishment.

hukuman mati
The legal killing of a person as punishment for a crime.
Related: death penalty, execution, abolish.
Many countries have abolished capital punishment.

remaja / anak di bawah umur
Relating to young people, especially in legal contexts.
Related: juvenile crime, youth, minor.
Juvenile offenders are usually sent to special centers.

pengawasan
Close observation, especially of suspects or public places.
Related: monitor, CCTV, watch.
CCTV surveillance has reduced crime in city centers.

hak-hak sipil
The rights of citizens to equality and freedom.
Related: human rights, civil liberties, equality.
The civil rights movement changed history in America.

kebijakan
A plan or course of action by a government or organization.
Related: policy maker, public policy, government policy.
The government introduced a new education policy.

pendanaan publik / dana pemerintah
Money provided by the government for public services.
Related: government spending, taxpayer money, subsidy.
Hospitals rely on public funding to operate.

konstitusi / undang-undang dasar
The basic principles by which a country is governed.
Related: constitutional, amendment, charter.
The constitution guarantees freedom of speech.

perpajakan
The system of charging taxes to citizens.
Related: tax, taxpayer, income tax.
High taxation can discourage business investment.

kesejahteraan / bantuan sosial
Government support for people in need.
Related: welfare state, benefits, social security.
The welfare system supports families on low incomes.
